metric_name: HP CIS market leadership — actual outcome
metric_value: Partially correct near-term; HP did execute Smart ContAct partnerships but CRM market consolidated around Siebel Systems (dominant 1997-2004) and Salesforce.com (founded 1999). HP exited CIS as distinct business.
notes: Aberdeen was optimistic: HP's Smart ContAct had traction in 1996-1998 but Siebel won CRM market. HP hardware remained relevant but not as CIS software leader. Salesforce disrupted entire sector.

metric_value: Aberdeen predicts CIS market will sustain 40%+ annual growth through mid-to-late 1990s, reaching multi-billion dollar scale
notes: Aberdeen research shows 40% growth already occurring in 1995; implicit prediction of continuation.

metric_value: Confirmed: CRM/CIS market grew explosively through late 1990s; Siebel Systems revenue grew from $119M (1996) to $1.4B (2000); Salesforce launched 1999; Gartner CRM market $23B by 2012
notes: Prediction confirmed: CRM market sustained 40%+ growth. Aberdeen correctly identified the market trajectory. CIS/CRM became one of largest enterprise software categories.

metric_name: CTI middleware critical to CIS prediction
metric_value: Aberdeen predicts CTI (Computer Telephony Integration) middleware will be a critical competitive differentiator in enterprise CIS architectures
notes: HP ACT/CCM positioned as key CTI middleware; Aberdeen frames CTI as essential CIS architectural layer.

metric_name: CTI middleware in CIS/CRM — actual outcome
metric_value: Confirmed: CTI integration remained central to call-center CRM through 2010s; evolved into cloud contact centers (Genesys, Avaya, Amazon Connect); Cisco UCCX became dominant CTI middleware by 2005
notes: Prediction confirmed: CTI middleware critical to CIS. HP ACT specifically was displaced by Cisco, Avaya, and cloud solutions but the architectural role was correct.

metric_value: Aberdeen predicts HP's partnerships with leading CIS ISVs (Clarify, Remedy, Scopus, Siebel, Vantive) will create a dominant ecosystem for enterprise CIS delivery
notes: Smart ContAct program built around 5 ISV partnerships; Aberdeen predicts this ecosystem strategy will cement HP's market position.

metric_name: CIS ISV partnership ecosystem — actual outcome
metric_value: Mixed: ISV ecosystem prediction correct as concept but wrong winner. Siebel dominated CRM (acquired by Oracle 2006 for $5.85B); Clarify acquired by Nortel 1998; Remedy acquired by BMC 2002; Vantive acquired by PeopleSoft 1999. HP not ecosystem winner.
notes: Aberdeen correctly identified the key ISVs (all became acquisition targets) but HP did not become the systems integrator hub. Each ISV was absorbed by larger software players.
